3:48:17 updates to highlight.

3:48:19 Last week, um, and this week, a busy

3:48:23 week in terms of public hearings and

3:48:24 board and committee votes on the

3:48:26 proposed warrant articles. Uh last week

3:48:29 on Monday, EDAB uh voted favorable

3:48:32 unanimously favorable action on special

3:48:34 town meeting one, but then um separately

3:48:36 they also endorsed a scope of friendly

3:48:38 amendments that staff presented which

3:48:39 I'm going to share with you this

3:48:40 evening.

3:48:42 Between Monday and Thursday, the scope

3:48:45 of what was included in those proposed

3:48:47 uh friendly amendments expanded to

3:48:48 include some of um the proposed

3:48:50 amendments that um Mr. Banka had

3:48:52 submitted. So the planning board and HAB

3:48:55 have met in a joint public hearing

3:48:56 regarding special town meeting warrant

3:48:58 article one. Um the planning board uh

3:49:01 voted in a vote of um six in support one

3:49:04 abstension for favorable action on the

3:49:05 zoning warrant article and endorsed um

3:49:07 unanimously the proposed set of friendly

3:49:09 amendments. They also adopted um subject

3:49:13 to town meeting approval the set of

3:49:15 design guidelines which is a companion

3:49:16 piece that the planning board will be

3:49:18 using as reference as they review

3:49:19 applications that come through uh the

3:49:21 Chesnut Hill Overlay District. Those

3:49:23 were approved unanimously. Housing

3:49:25 advisory board unanimous vote for

3:49:26 favorable action on special town meeting

3:49:28 one and then also unanim unanimously

3:49:31 endorsed uh the scope of amendments

3:49:33 presented.

3:49:35 Upcoming public hearings. Last night we

3:49:37 were at uh advisory land use

3:49:39 subcommittee. That meeting has been

3:49:40 continued to April 22nd. Um we have

3:49:43 backto-back public hearing commitments

3:49:44 on April 22nd. We'll also be going to

3:50:23 select board have received from property

3:50:25 owners within the Chesnut Hill

3:50:26 commercial area. So, just for the sake

3:50:48 requirements in the particularly in the

3:51:35 they intend to follow through with that

4:20:09 the zoning code thereby losing our

4:20:17 meeting last night where Cara Brutin was

4:20:19 there. Um the team is doing great. We do

4:20:22 have a vision. We're rallying around a

4:20:24 vision uh for comprehensive planning.

4:20:29 parcel, corridor. We looked at the

4:20:31 entire town. There's three areas uh of

4:20:34 uh that we're classifying. I'm gonna

4:20:37 boil them down to three. I think there

4:20:38 were six, but they're all related. The

4:20:40 first is an area where we would conserve

4:20:42 and maintain. Think of neighborhoods

4:20:46 that you would see some development ADUs

4:20:48 and stuff, but it wouldn't be

4:20:49 transformative. Um, expanding. This

4:20:52 would be infill development similar to

4:20:54 what we uh zoned for Harvard Street.

4:20:56 Think Beacon Street, think Route 9, um

4:21:00 uh you know uh Washington Square, uh

4:21:03 Puttingham Circle. These are areas where

4:21:06 um it wouldn't be transformative meaning

4:21:09 it would be a completely different use

4:21:11 but it would be infill and expansion. Um

4:21:13 and then the last is transformative

4:21:15 areas and that transformation is very

4:21:18 limited. There's only a few areas within

4:21:21 Brookline where you could truly

4:21:22 transform what's there. The areas on

4:21:25 comm over by Target, we've talked about

4:21:27 that before. That's an area of

4:21:29 tremendous opportunity transformation.

4:21:31 Um, Chestnut Hill West is another area

4:21:34 of potential major transformation, Stop

4:21:37 and Shop, uh, TJ Maxx, but there's very

4:21:40 few of these parcels and, um, my concern

4:21:45 about giving away,

4:21:48 uh, it's one thing to do the ex if we

4:21:55 for the entire corridor of Beacon

4:21:57 Street, which is something that we

4:21:58 discussed last night, instead of doing

4:22:00 it neighborhood by neighborhood, do an

4:22:02 entire corridor, you would get infill

4:22:04 development and you wouldn't need to

4:22:05 have a tax certainty agreement. But when

4:22:08 you get to major parcels like Chestnut

4:22:10 Hill West, you want to have the leverage

4:22:12 of zoning to get what you want out of

4:22:15 that negotiation. And I think that we

4:22:18 it's a principle actually that frankly

4:31:50 package means that there is by estimate

4:31:54 of the traffic consultant the potential

4:31:56 for 300% increase in trips. um then

4:32:00 maybe we're not ready to go ahead with

4:32:01 the whole thing and we better take a

4:32:04 little bit of cautious approach to this

4:32:06 and maybe just go ahead with the city

4:32:08 realy part and and hopefully find out

4:32:12 what the nobody has said that we have

4:32:16 that 300% uh increase in trips.

4:32:20 Okay, quick Mike and then then we're

4:32:22 open up the uh hearing.

4:32:24 I just want to point out John that

4:32:26 that's 300% over 20 years. So when you

4:32:28 say we haven't figured it out, you also

4:32:31 have to acknowledge we have 20 years to

4:32:35 to work on this problem.

4:32:37 Okay. So let's hope so.

4:32:39 Sorry, I'm so sorry. Just one additional

4:32:41 so that I think the

4:32:42 the flip side to that is that you know

4:32:47 we the I think the benefit in

4:32:50 undertaking this transportation study is

4:32:51 that we are starting conversations with

4:32:54 mass DOT that much farther along. We

4:32:57 have done our homework. We've identified

4:32:58 a scope of possibilities, what we think

4:33:00 could work, and at least one

4:33:01 intersection redesign option at Boilston

4:33:03 and Hammond Street that would

4:33:04 significantly mitigate the anticipated

4:33:06 traffic impacts that we were just

4:33:07 talking about

4:33:09 in order to continue those

4:33:11 conversations. If we only move forward

4:33:13 with a portion of the proposed zoning

4:33:16 and the rest of the study area remains

4:33:18 unclear, it's going to make continuing

4:33:20 those conversations with Mass DOT about

4:33:22 what interventions are needed in this

4:33:24 area that much harder to advance because

4:33:26 we don't know. Um, and we're going to

4:33:28 and it sounds like, you know, just

4:33:30 within the bounds of this conversation,

4:33:31 there's the interest in maybe deferring

4:33:33 to, you know, when developers are ready

4:33:35 and that could take decades. So, I think

4:33:37 um almost in service to wanting to be

4:33:41 able to, you know, be more proactive and

4:33:44 aggressive in um advancing

4:33:46 infrastructure improvements, they're

4:33:47 going to support the development we need

4:33:49 for economic development. It would be

4:33:50 helpful to keep the zoning overlay

4:33:52 intact so we can plan accordingly.

4:34:24 All right. Well, good evening. Good

4:34:26 evening everyone. Um thank you for

4:34:30 little context and overview from EDAB.

4:34:33 Um you know I've sat on EDAB for a

4:34:35 couple years now. Um and we've worked

4:34:38 really hard for two years trying to

4:34:41 finesse a resolution to find common

4:34:44 ground a bunch of very varied

4:34:46 stakeholders. Um you know one respecting

4:34:49 the community the neighborhood number

4:34:51 two maximizing the position from you

4:34:56 revenue. Number three, understanding

4:35:05 forces, right? What's with all the

4:35:07 oscillations, the headwinds that come up

4:35:08 and down, how do we position this site

4:35:11 for success, right? And I think that the

4:35:13 Warren article at hand does position

4:35:15 this site for success. And right now,

4:35:17 it's clearly underutilized and and

4:35:19 undervalued.

4:35:21 Um so that's you know just kind of the

4:35:23 the overview at least from you know

4:35:25 working on a EDAB subcommittee um you

4:35:28 know with all all parties at hand. Um

4:35:31 also just to address kind of the the the

4:35:33 bigger picture when we're saying about

4:35:35 you do one parcel versus many parcels.

4:35:37 Um you know we've brought in a couple

4:35:39 different brokers right and and and I've

4:35:41 been personally in real estate

4:35:42 development for about 25 years. Um

4:35:45 uncertainty is always the enemy of of

4:35:46 good economic development, right? I

4:35:48 think um if it's a peacemail approach,

4:35:51 one parcel at a time, um you're just

4:35:54 making it that much more difficult for,

4:35:56 you know, a a prominent um and quality

4:35:59 development. You know, if it's a big

4:36:00 tenant with lots of jobs wants to come

4:36:02 in and you're not sure, well, did the

4:36:04 guys next door, am I going to get what

4:36:05 they got? I don't know. Maybe, maybe

4:36:07 not. You're just adding more headwind,

4:36:10 you know, to I think to to the entire um

4:36:12 you know, problem at hand. So again, I'm

4:36:15 I'm would just in and just good best

4:36:17 practices when you look at other, you

4:36:19 know, neighboring towns, upzoning is the

4:36:21 main tool and through a special permit,

4:36:23 those are always the hooks, right? And

4:36:25 and the hooks to make sure that you get

4:36:27 the right agreements for the development

4:36:28 downstream. Um, so I just think again

4:36:33 the ability to zone the consistent way

4:36:35 which is a clear and distinct message to

4:36:37 the marketplace be it brokers, tenants

4:36:39 and whoever else um I think is loud and

4:36:42 clear and I think we'll at least

4:36:44 facilitate as best we can and position

4:36:46 the site for success.

4:40:45 Okay, next uh speaker. We have someone.

4:40:48 Um we have three people online raising

4:40:50 their hand.

4:40:50 Okay,

4:40:51 Kath is first.

4:40:52 Katha,

4:40:58 good evening. Thank you for letting me

4:41:00 speak. Um my name is Kathy Seidman. I'm

4:41:03 a town meeting member in precinct 2, a

4:41:05 member of the Brooklyn Commission for

4:41:07 the Arts, and a board member of Brooklyn

4:41:09 for Everyone. But I speak here only for

4:41:11 myself. The Chestnut Hill commercial

4:41:14 area is the largest and most promising

4:41:16 commercial area in Brooklyn and holds

4:41:18 enormous potential for tax generating

4:41:22 development that includes a mix of

4:41:23 commercial uses as well as housing at

4:41:26 all price points. Adopting new zoning

4:41:28 regulations that allow this site to

4:41:30 achieve its full potential pres presents

4:41:32 an opportunity that must not be

4:41:34 squandered by the town, especially now

4:41:37 when a developer that owns the requisite

4:41:39 size parcel has presented a specific

4:41:42 proposal proposal to actually build out

4:41:44 that potential. Passing the zoning

4:41:47 outline in STM1 warrant articles 1 and

4:41:49 two would not only allow the proposed

4:41:52 development of 5.3 acres of Chestnut

4:41:54 Hill commercial area to move forward,

4:41:56 but obviously incentivizes other

4:41:59 developers to come forward. Brookline

4:42:01 has a history of making new development

4:42:03 impossible by never changing it zoning.

4:42:06 expecting a developer to ask for zoning

4:42:09 to meet a developer's program could as

4:42:11 easily as not run into the same 20-year

4:42:14 process roadblocks as the city realy

4:42:16 property encountered. I want to

4:42:19 congratulate all those who worked on

4:42:20 this project from CHCA study committee,

4:42:23 the neighbors, staff, and select board

4:42:25 members who all worked tirelessly to

4:42:27 upgrade a stagnant Chestnut Hill office

4:42:29 park and reszone the other areas

4:42:31 proposed in the 2005 comp plan into what

4:42:34 has the potential to be a placemaking

4:42:37 model for future developments of this

4:42:39 scale in Brooklyn. I would like to also

4:42:42 take this opportunity

4:42:44 to point [clears throat] out what I

4:42:46 believe has been an overlooked benefit

4:42:47 of this upgrade to Brooklyn side of

4:42:50 Route 9.

4:42:52 Our developers to our west, Newton,

4:42:54 Welssley, and as far away as South

4:42:56 Burough, have all added residential and

4:42:58 commercial development along Route 9.

4:43:00 The traffic those residents and shoppers

4:43:02 generate flows through Brooklyn to

4:43:05 Boston and back, adding traffic and

4:43:07 congestion to Brooklyn. Those towns get

4:43:10 increased property tax revenue from

4:43:12 their new developments. Brooklyn gets

4:43:14 the traffic. We can and should take

4:43:17 advantage of the revenue from both the

4:43:19 new development of homes for folks who

4:43:21 want to live closer to Boston and the

4:43:23 economic activity generated by folks

4:43:25 visiting Brookline's new placemaking

4:43:28 experience. In particular, I am

4:43:31 delighted to learn that the neighbors

4:43:33 and the developer and the town have

4:43:35 reached consensus on amendments to the

4:43:37 original zoning. I hope the advisory

4:43:39 committee, the select board, and every

4:43:40 town meeting member will now fully

4:43:42 support these articles. I urge this I

4:43:45 urge the select board to recommend

4:43:47 favorable favorable action on STDM

4:43:50 warrant articles one and two. Thank you.

4:46:59 where that can happen um beyond city

4:47:03 reality is uh the growth companies.

4:47:19 Ann Meyers is next.

4:47:24 Thank you. I'm Anne Meyers. I live in

4:47:27 Precinct 9 and I chair the Economic

4:47:29 Development Advisory Board. Um, I

4:47:32 actually live in precinct 8 now. I keep

4:47:34 forgetting. I moved from one to the

4:47:35 other not that long ago. Um, I was

4:47:38 actually on the 20 uh 20 2005, excuse

4:47:42 me, uh, comp plan committee and I

4:47:46 remember when the res need for resoning

4:47:49 at Chestnut Hill was raised in that comp

4:47:51 plan and was discussed uh, quite a lot

4:47:54 and was one of the elements of that

4:47:56 plan. Since then, the town has responded

4:47:59 to development proposals on a

4:48:00 case-bycase basis. And people, including

4:48:03 many town meeting members, always

4:48:05 complain that Brooklyn just responds to

4:48:07 proposals and never got ahead of the

4:48:09 game by issuing resoning in advance of

4:48:12 proposals. Uh, the Chesnut Hill

4:48:15 commercial area study started as a way

4:48:17 to look at the entire area and develop

4:48:19 resoning before there were any

4:48:21 development proposals on the table.

4:48:23 Having zoning in place lets a

4:48:25 development community know what the town

4:48:27 is looking for and what they can do by

4:48:29 right, even for sites that are unlikely

4:48:31 to be developed for many years. In this

4:48:34 case, part of the site was purchased and

4:48:36 a proposal submitted just as the work

4:48:38 was beginning, which did change some of

4:48:40 the focus of the work, but it did not

4:48:42 eliminate the need for the entire site

4:48:44 to be zoned. Now, if it is, it will make

4:48:47 it much more desirable for developers to

4:48:49 come in and start a project in the area.

4:48:52 one that helps to generate the

4:48:54 commercial tax revenue that Brookline so

4:48:55 desperately needs. And again, I I want

4:49:00 to urge the select board to support

4:49:02 these warrant articles and thank all the

4:49:05 work that's been done by the staff and

4:49:08 the committees and everybody else. This

4:49:10 has been several years of very hard work

4:49:12 and I hope that we can bring it to a

4:49:14 conclusion. Thank you.

4:52:19 know my opportunities to provide comment

5:01:40 uh these are revenue sources. Uh CPA is

5:01:49 1% sir charge on real estate property

5:01:52 tax bills. Um the state community

5:01:56 preservation trust fund provides us a

5:02:04 that are paid at the registry. That's

5:02:07 what community preservation act funds uh

5:02:10 uh get as a state match and uh and

5:04:02 preservation, open space and recreation.

5:04:35 and 32% in open space and recreation.

5:04:41 in community housing, behind a bit in

5:05:44 when the state match and the interest

5:06:16 million and the 10% uh budget reserves

5:06:35 second motion is our administrative

5:06:44 those include salaries uh dues to the

5:06:47 state organization from which we get a

5:06:53 also pro uh gives us the the funds to

5:07:19 lose the money. It we it always stays

5:07:22 with us. Uh and but it goes back into

5:07:25 our unallocated budget reserve for

5:19:02 when it has not created the actual uh

5:19:05 apartments to begin with. In addition,

5:19:08 as I say, we've done we've done u

5:19:11 projects for the BHA roof preservation,

5:19:14 uh window restoration, that kind of

5:19:16 thing, which are very important to

5:19:18 tenants because they prevent le they

5:19:21 keep keep them dry and uh also keep the

5:19:25 energy costs down and the feel of how

5:19:28 they what it feels like to live in an

5:19:30 apartment where the wind is blowing in.

5:19:32 Uh so we're cognizant of that and we are

5:19:36 um

5:19:38 working on that. HAB has not really done

5:19:42 that to the extent that uh at least

5:19:46 since I've been involved in it since um

5:19:50 uh CPA has come into existence. And I

5:19:53 think that's a good role for us to play

5:19:56 because I think it's important for us to

5:19:58 support this housing that we have and to

5:20:02 um ensure that our tenants the tenants

5:20:05 in those buildings and they're not town

5:20:08 uh owned. They are the BHA is stateowned

5:20:13 agency since we vote for the

5:20:14 commissioners but uh and the BCDC is uh

5:20:19 uh a nonprofit uh that's operating in

5:20:22 the town but these are town residents

5:20:25 and they deserve to live in uh decent

5:20:28 conditions and uh I think it's you know

5:20:32 certainly morally um correct to uh

5:20:37 pursue this path.

5:20:38 of making sure that the tenants we have

5:20:41 are in are living in good conditions as

5:20:44 well as building more units for tenants,

5:20:48 prospective tenants.

5:21:40 but you could try.

5:22:22 it would require a unique situation and

5:25:29 available for housing uh purposes. at a

5:25:33 minimum we need to have 10% for housing

5:25:37 purposes. So the difference in that has

5:25:42 purposes. Um, and I mean in theory you

5:25:47 could use more of those unallocated

5:25:49 purposes for things other than housing,

5:37:14 you're explain why Marty.

5:37:16 Yeah. Okay. So, I I have been saying for

5:37:20 a couple of weeks that I don't

5:37:22 understand why the petitioners are want

5:37:26 to run the risk of having a bylaw

5:37:29 reviewed by the attorney general and

5:37:32 voiding not only this bylaw but also all

5:37:36 the executive orders by by implication.

5:37:38 I think and the reasons are I've stated

5:37:42 them before. I think it's loaded with

5:37:44 legal issues and the town council memo I

5:37:47 thought was very cursory saying that

5:37:50 paragraph one was okay. Even that one

5:37:53 has issues aside from the ICE issues.

5:37:56 And I hope anyone has looked at the Vera

5:37:59 report that I talked I've talked about

5:38:01 and cited for you that talks about the

5:38:03 ICE issues, the sovereign immunity

5:38:06 versus the 10th amendment. But there's

5:38:09 also issues about essentially a trespass

5:38:12 law. Doesn't call it a trespass law. And

5:38:15 I think that's probably preempted by

5:38:17 state law. I don't think it's adequately

5:38:20 enforceable. I think it's still got a

5:38:22 lot of vagueness, which criminal laws uh

5:38:26 scrutinize for vagueness. And the

5:38:29 enforcement provision that's been

5:38:31 changed is just not going to work. I'm

5:38:34 sorry. Uh, you cannot

5:38:37 make 10.1, you can't overrule it. 10.1

5:38:42 says a violation of the bylaws is a

5:38:44 misdemeanor. And that comes from state

5:38:46 law. Last week, uh, the chief and I

5:38:49 think Chi-Chi agreed that it's a general

5:38:52 law. It's not a general law. It's state

5:38:54 law that goes back to common law. In

5:38:56 fact, it goes back to for any lawyers in

5:38:59 the room, Blackstone's commentaries that

5:39:02 that anything that is deemed to be a

5:39:05 violation against the state a state of

5:39:09 or where the state is the plaintiff is a

5:39:13 crime. It's a misdemeanor. And that's

5:39:15 where 10.1 comes for. This bylaw

5:39:18 doesn't, the draft doesn't try to

5:39:20 overrule uh 10.1 and the enforcement

5:39:24 provision that civil enforcement that's

5:39:27 already in our bylaws that something can

5:39:29 be enforced civily, but that doesn't

5:39:31 take it out of the designation as a as a

5:39:34 misdemeanor and a crime that gets

5:39:36 scrutinized by the attorney general. So,

5:39:38 I just think that paragraph one, there's

5:39:41 other issues as well. says, "I brought

5:39:44 up the streets and sidewalks issues, but

5:39:46 what about parking lots and and parks?

5:39:49 If the police see someone they want to

5:39:52 kidnap a alleged detainee in a park or

5:39:58 uh in a parking lot, are we saying they

5:40:01 can't go in there?" That's going to run

5:40:03 into the the sovereign immunity issues

5:40:06 and and the police station is now

5:40:08 considered banned. They can't go into

5:40:11 the police station and ask if someone is

5:40:13 in detention or when they're coming out.

5:40:15 I have proposed last week that we add a

5:40:20 a general order along the lines of the

5:40:22 Plymouth provision that John Van Goyak

5:40:25 sent me uh which is that our our in

5:40:29 keeping with our sanctuary order the

5:40:31 desk officer could say I'm not

5:40:33 authorized to tell you anything. But

5:40:34 that's a separate issue. But the point

5:40:36 is, how can we make the police station

5:40:39 out of bounds for ICE? The other thing

5:40:42 about this is that Alec brought up last

5:40:45 week that we want it to be that you

5:40:47 folks and I do too. I want something

5:40:50 more binding to so that people can count

5:40:53 on it. But the fact of the matter is, no

5:40:56 matter what happens here, ICE is not

5:40:59 going to care about what we say in

5:41:00 Brooklyn. And the fact is that people

5:41:03 should not be told, as Alec said last

5:41:05 week, with all due respect, I know it

5:41:07 was well motivated, that people should

5:41:10 be are counting on this. Nobody should

5:41:12 be counting on this. And I I hope that

5:41:15 the petitioners are not telling people

5:41:18 that

5:41:19 even the proclamation

5:41:22 is is going to save them from ICE right

5:41:24 now. It isn't. In fact, ICE doesn't care

5:41:27 about homicide laws and kidnapping laws.

5:41:30 So, why are they going to care about an

5:41:32 a trespass law that's not called that?

5:41:35 And and lastly, um I I know that uh you

5:41:39 want to get on and get get the meeting

5:41:40 over with, but um the the uh the the

5:41:45 definition of the uh oh, by the way, the

5:41:48 it's there's a case called Sherman

5:41:50 Manufacturing 1905 that says a uh crime

5:41:54 or misdemeanor is an act committed or

5:41:57 omitted in violation of the public law.

5:42:01 So that's where the Blackstone's

5:42:03 commentary comes in that this is going

5:42:05 to be a crime no matter how you slice it

5:42:07 and the police shall enforce town

5:42:11 bylaws. So you're putting them in a

5:42:13 bind. What are they supposed to do with

5:42:15 this?

5:42:16 The bottom line is I really believe that

5:42:19 this would create a monkey wrench for

5:42:22 the at least six cities that now have

5:42:26 executive orders and maybe the governors

5:42:28 as well because some of the language is

5:42:30 the same as the governors. And I don't

5:42:32 think that's a risk worth taking. I

5:42:34 think it should be a study not by the

5:42:37 board but a committee created by the

5:42:39 board and that it should act quickly and

5:42:43 try to revise especially the general

5:42:45 orders. Whether there should be a bylaw,

5:42:52 so just the uh portion you mentioned,

5:43:45 department. Um, we did, uh, as I said,

5:44:02 um, I mean, the the obligation of the

5:44:36 that's right. And I take the point that

5:49:45 have more legal weight so that it's

5:49:50 Um and you know the idea is that all

5:52:03 Um, is it okay if I share my screen uh

5:52:05 and show slides with the edits we've

5:52:08 made?

5:52:08 Yes, go ahead.

5:52:11 Okay. And I'll be as brief as humanly

5:52:13 possible.

5:52:15 Uh so again in response to feedback that

5:52:19 we received both um before the select

5:52:22 board and in public hearing before a

5:52:24 subcommittee of the advisory um council

5:52:27 we've made three specific edits um to

5:52:30 the local planks of warrant article 25.

5:52:33 Um the first one is with regards to

5:52:35 fingerprinting. Um our resolution calls

5:52:39 for not fingerprinting juveniles or

5:52:41 following arrest for misdemeanors. Um,

5:52:43 and there were some concerns expressed

5:52:45 by our uh, police department um, that

5:52:49 doing so would mean that we would not be

5:52:51 in compliance with state police

5:52:53 accredititation standards. Um, this is

5:52:56 not an area of expertise for me. The

5:52:57 research that I found indicates that

5:52:59 state law requires fingerprinting only

5:53:01 for felonies. Um, and I couldn't find

5:53:03 anything specific in our police

5:53:05 accreditation that requires

5:53:06 fingerprinting across the board for

5:53:08 every arrest. Uh, nonetheless, we

5:53:11 included um this qualifying language

5:53:13 unless required by state law or police

5:53:16 accredititation um to address those

5:53:18 concerns.

5:53:20 The second uh is that we also added a

5:53:23 qualifier to applying for UASI grants

5:53:26 and we added that we shall cease

5:53:29 applying for grants unless such grants

5:53:31 do not require cooperation, support of

5:53:34 or data sharing with federal immigration

5:53:36 enforcement or border crisis response.

5:53:39 participation in the brick or

5:53:42 cooperation with federal election

5:53:44 interference.

5:53:45 And then the last one, not as

5:53:47 substantive, we struck um the names of

5:53:50 the individuals in the very last uh

5:53:53 bullet um and instead just referred to

5:53:56 uh officers by title and removed

5:53:59 reference to the select board and the

5:54:01 police chief as they will of course um

5:54:04 be aware of the warrant article. So

5:54:06 those are our three uh recommended ch uh

5:54:11 suggested edits uh in response to the

5:54:14 feedback that we've received.

6:09:17 I think there's a lot of there's a lot

6:09:19 of confusion which I understand. Um not

6:09:21 everybody has been living the brick for

6:09:24 15 years or whatever as long as I have I

6:09:26 guess longer. Um Uassi and the brick are

6:09:29 not the same thing. There was some

6:09:31 information um you know in some of the

6:09:35 public hearings people came forward.

6:09:39 testified that you know she lives in

6:09:40 Newton. Newton's a safe city. Newton's

6:09:42 not part of the brick. And that's simply

6:09:43 not true. I think that we're all in

6:09:45 agreement that law enforcement, the

6:09:46 Brooklyn Police Department, uh,

6:09:48 specifically, you know, we need our

6:09:49 partners. We need our relationships with

6:09:53 effective in keeping our community safe.

6:09:55 The brick is operations. The brick is

6:09:58 how we do that. Um, the brick extends

6:10:01 far beyond the UAS, the nine cities and

6:10:03 towns that make up. I can't think of a a

6:10:06 single city or town in our immediate

6:10:08 area that doesn't contribute to the

6:10:10 brick, including the colleges and

6:10:11 universities, Newton, the other, you

6:10:13 know, surrounding cities and towns. Um,

6:10:16 it's not, it's more than just the UASI

6:10:18 cities and towns. And I understand why

6:10:19 that's confusing. Um, UASI funds the

6:10:22 brick, but the brick serves as a

6:10:23 regional hub similar to the Commonwealth

6:10:25 Fusion Center. Those are the two for the

6:10:27 state. Um so we would be removing

6:10:30 ourselves

6:10:32 from the [snorts] hub of information um

6:10:34 gathering you know collecting and and in

6:10:37 um you know synthesizing and sharing out

6:10:41 it just it operationally wouldn't make

6:10:43 any sense for us and we don't have the

6:10:46 capacity and it just wouldn't be good

6:10:48 practice to try to replicate that on our

6:10:49 own. Um there were a few other things

6:10:53 mentioned. You know, I think somebody

6:10:55 talked about the brick, for example,

6:10:56 being present at

6:10:59 um Occupy Boston. I mean, those things,

6:11:02 you know, you can take there's a little

6:11:03 bit of truth in everything that I think

6:11:04 was said. Occupy Boston, I remember back

6:11:07 in the day there were there was crime

6:11:08 there. It was they weren't there to

6:11:10 impede on First Amendment rights. They

6:11:12 were there because there was drug open

6:11:13 market, open air drug dealing happening,

6:11:15 sexual assaults were happening.

6:11:18 um it it was becoming violent towards

6:11:20 the end and so you know a police

6:11:22 presence was warranted. Um and I think

6:11:25 with regard to you know the statistics

6:11:27 that are being shared um regarding

6:11:29 demographics we should be troubled by

6:11:32 that. But I have to say you know if you

6:11:34 go on the Boston Police Department

6:11:35 they're pretty transparent. They have a

6:11:37 crime dashboard. They share out their

6:11:39 homicide statistics. And if you look at

6:11:40 the demographics of who's being killed

6:11:42 in instances of gang violence in the

6:11:44 city, the statistics and the

6:11:46 demographics are a pretty close match.

6:11:48 And that's unfortunate. That's something

6:11:49 to be solved for. But illuminating us

6:11:52 from the brick does nothing to solve for
